L1.area: Data and resource downloading, packaging, and extraction utilities

L2.description: A path traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability exists in the archive extraction logic of a resource downloading component. The extraction routine uses the standard library's extractall() method without validating member file paths against the intended extraction directory. An attacker can craft a malicious ZIP archive containing entries with directory traversal sequences that write files outside the target directory. If the archive includes executable files such as Python modules, they may be automatically loaded and executed upon import, leading to remote code execution.

L3.area: NLP corpus and resource downloader and its ZIP archive extraction logic
L3.description: A path traversal (Zip Slip) vulnerability exists in the ZIP extraction routine of the NLP corpus and resource downloader. The extraction function calls extractall() without validating that member file paths resolve within the intended extraction root directory, allowing crafted ZIP entries with traversal sequences or absolute paths to write files to arbitrary locations. If a malicious NLP data package contains Python modules, they are automatically imported and executed, resulting in remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The _unzip_iter function in the NLTK downloader uses zipfile.extractall() without validating member file paths, allowing a crafted ZIP archive to write files outside the intended extraction directory via path traversal sequences, leading to arbitrary code execution.
